behavior_summary: U8P_STALAG::look is a short U8P_STALAG::look/description handler that displays the single text 'stalactite' when the player examines the object.

U8P_STALAG::hit handles the object being struck and implements progressive damage: it queries the current frame, branches by frame thresholds, distinguishes NPC vs non-NPC hitters, applies hits (via Item::receiveHit or METHOD::receiveHitWrapper), plays sound effects, may set the avatar in stasis briefly, updates the item's type/frame, and ultimately destroys the item when damage exceeds a threshold.

The U8P_STALAG::hit logic increments a local frame/damage counter, spawns a short delay helper (spawn ->FREE::func1C19) before suspending, and uses random variation (urandom) to alter U8P_STALAG::hit effects.
notable_control_flow: U8P_STALAG::hit first reads Item::getFrame(this) and chooses different branches based on the current frame; at least two threshold comparisons are used (inferred <= 0x0A and <= 0x04) to select progressive-damage behavior.

Within branches, the code checks Item::isNpc(addressof(param1)) to select NPC-specific handling vs non-NPC handling and further compares the hitter reference (param1/local5) to 0x01h for a special-case path.

NPC hitters use Item::receiveHit(this, attacker, ...) while non-NPC hitters use METHOD::receiveHitWrapper or alternate engine wrappers; calls are accompanied by playSFX and setAvatarInStasis(0x01) in some branches.

After applying U8P_STALAG::hit effects the script sets item state (Item::setType(0x02FCh) and Item::setFrame(local1,this)), increments local1, spawns a small helper process, and suspend()s; once local1 exceeds thresholds the item is destroyed (Item::destroy).
open_questions: Exact numeric semantics of the frame thresholds (<= 0x0A and <= 0x04) and how they map to visual stages or durability are inferred but not certain.
Precise meaning of the second parameter (word) to U8P_STALAG::hit — likely damage type/flag but not confirmed.
Purpose and gameplay consequence of changing the item type to 0x02FC (Item::setType) — likely transforms behaviour/appearance but exact effect is unknown.
Role of the special-case compare against 0x01h for param1/local5: whether this represents the avatar, a particular actor, or a sentinel value is unclear.
spawn ->FREE::func1C19 is used as a short delay helper before suspend; its exact behavior/timeout is not determined from this script alone.

main_function.name: U8P_STALAG::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown when the player examines the stalactite object (barks 'stalactite').
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player performs the U8P_STALAG::look/examine action on this item.

function_map.name: U8P_STALAG::hit
function_map.role: U8P_STALAG::hit/damage handler for the object (progressive damage, apply hits, change frame/type, destroy)

function_map.name: Item::receiveHit
function_map.role: engine API to apply damage when attacker is an NPC

function_map.name: METHOD::receiveHitWrapper
function_map.role: engine wrapper used to apply hits for non-NPC hitters or alternate handling

external_calls.purpose: Query current visual/state frame to decide which damage branch to run.
Determine whether the hitter reference is an NPC to choose NPC-specific U8P_STALAG::hit logic.
Temporarily set or clear avatar stasis around certain U8P_STALAG::hit sequences (0x01 to set, 0x00 to clear).
Play branch-specific sound effects when the item is U8P_STALAG::hit.
Refresh/reset an engine reference (Kernel::resetRef(0x0203h,this)) before/after interactions.
Apply damage directly when the attacker is an NPC (passes damage flags, amount, urandom).
Apply damage via an engine wrapper for non-NPC hitters; includes random variation.
Change the item's type (observed: 0x02FCh) after certain hits to alter behavior/appearance.
Update the item's frame index to represent progressive damage states (uses local1).
Remove the item from the world when damage/frame thresholds are exceeded.
Generate small random variation used in damage/U8P_STALAG::hit calls.
Spawn a short-lived helper process or delay prior to suspend; used to schedule a small timeout.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_STALAG::look is a short U8P_STALAG::look/description handler that displays the single text 'stalactite' when the player examines the object.
